message:
harden(core): saturating arithmetic in fd_budget + bounds-safe accessors in shortest_path

Two independent but parallel defensive-programming fixes to remove panic
and overflow edges from hot utility code.

── fd_budget.rs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Every arithmetic operation over the FD-budget totals is now saturating,
so a runaway pane count, a misconfigured `fds_per_pane`, or a hostile
re-registration can no longer wrap `total_allocated` or the validator's
`required_fds` / `required_system_fds` values through zero.

- `validate_system_limits`: `target_panes * fds_per_pane` →
  `saturating_mul`; the doubled "2x safety margin" is hoisted into a
  dedicated `required_system_fds` binding (also saturating) and reused
  for the check, the reported required value, and the printed sysctl
  fix command, so all three agree under overflow instead of silently
  wrapping.
- `FdBudget::can_admit_pane`: `current + fds_per_pane` →
  `saturating_add`, so admission always refuses at the cap instead of
  wrapping to 0 and falsely passing the usage check.
- `FdBudget::register_pane`: was unconditional `fetch_add`, which
  double-counted a re-registration and underflowed on the subsequent
  `unregister_pane`. Now inspects the prior value returned by
  `PaneMap::insert` and only adjusts `total_allocated` by the delta
  (or leaves it alone when the same allocation is re-inserted). Wraps
  the add/subtract in new `add_allocated` / `subtract_allocated`
  helpers that use `fetch_update` with saturating ops, so concurrent
  overflow or underflow is impossible.
- `unregister_pane`: routed through `subtract_allocated` for the same
  saturating guarantee.
- Platform identification extracted into `platform_identifier()` that
  also reports `"windows"` and `"unknown"` rather than mis-labelling
  them as `"unix"`.

New tests cover the behavioural changes directly:

- `duplicate_register_then_unregister_returns_to_zero` — the reason the
  re-registration delta logic matters.
- `can_admit_pane_saturates_projected_fd_count` and
  `register_pane_saturates_total_allocated_on_overflow` — force
  `total_allocated` near `u64::MAX` and assert saturation rather than
  wraparound.
- `validate_limits_saturates_required_fd_counts` — ditto for the
  validator's multiplied totals.

Also cleaned up a handful of `match { .. => {}, other => panic! }`
blocks into `assert!(matches!(..))` / destructuring-with-assert for
readability; no behavioural change in those tests.

── shortest_path.rs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

All index-based accessors on `WeightedGraph` and `ShortestPathResult`
now go through bounds-checked `.get()` instead of `[]`, so an
out-of-range node id (or a stale result consulted against a graph that
has since shrunk) returns sensible "unreachable" / empty values instead
of panicking:

- `WeightedGraph::neighbors(u)` returns `&[]` for out-of-range `u`.
- `ShortestPathResult::distance_to(target)` returns `f64::INFINITY`
  for out-of-range `target`.
- `ShortestPathResult::is_reachable(target)` returns false for OOB.
- `ShortestPathResult::path_to(target)` guards against OOB target,
  OOB parent links discovered during reconstruction, and path length
  exceeding `prev.len()` (which would indicate a cycle in `prev`).
  It also verifies the reconstructed path actually terminates at
  `self.source` and returns `None` otherwise.
- `dijkstra` and `bellman_ford` short-circuit via a new
  `unreachable_result()` helper when `source >= n`, and skip neighbour
  edges whose target `v >= n` instead of indexing into `dist`/`prev`.

Net effect: the shortest-path utilities are now total functions over
`usize` inputs — no panic paths remain — while preserving all existing
behaviour for valid inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
